State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5660 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444

April 16, 1982

Mr. Ray E. Cunningham
2201 The Alameda #18
Santa Clara, CA 95050

Dear Mr. Cunningham:

This is in reply to your letter dated March 31, 1982, concerning the campaign statement filed Citizens for Jack Komar, covering the period 1/1/82 through 3/17/82. Your question concerned whether "The Holley Trust" was adequately identified as a lender on Schedule B.

The Political Reform Act requires the name and address, occupation and employer, and date and amount of each contribution (including a loan received from persons who have contributed $100 or more. There is no requirement that a business entity or trust making a contribution be identified in any greater detail.

The Commission staff intends to consider the possibility of requiring further identification of business entities and trusts, such as the nature of the business entity, or the name of the administrator of the trust, or the person responsible for making the decision to make a contribution. However, no such requirement exists at this time.

Sincerely,

Jeanne Pritchard
Political Reform Consultant

JP:sc

Ray E. Cunningham
2201 The Alameda #18
Santa Clara, California 95050

March 31, 1982


Fair Political Practices Commission
State of California
1100 Kay Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen:

Enclosed is a copy of Schedule B, page 4, of the
Consolidated Campaign Statement of Citizens for Jack
Komar, I.D. #820003.

I am writing to ask if the $10,000 loan from
"The Holley Trust" constitutes disclosure as required
by FPPC regulations. It certainly does not inform
the public of the actual source of the funds.

Your prompt inquiry and reply will be most
appreciated.

Very truly yours,


RAY E. CUNNINGHAM

Your question concerned whether "The Holley Trust" was adequately identified as a lender on Schedule B. There is no requirement that a business entity or trust making a contribution be identified in any greater detail. The Commission staff intends to consider the possibility of requiring further identification of business entities and trusts, such as the nature of the business entity, or the name of the administrator of the trust, or the person responsible for making the decision to make a contribution. However, no such requirement exists at this time. Sincerely, Jeanne Pritchard Political Reform Consultant Ray E. CANDIDATE/OFFICEHOLDER INCLUDED IN THIS CONSOLIDATED REPORT:** - **Name of Candidate:** Jack Komar - **Office Sought or Held:** District Attorney-Santa Clara Co - **Residential Address:** 1198 Naval Way, San Jose, California 95125 - **Business Address:** 278 North Second Street, San Jose, California 95112 **II. COMMITTEES INCLUDED IN THIS CONSOLIDATED REPORT:** - **Committee Name:** Citizens for Jack Komar for District Attorney - **Address of Committee:** c/o R. Rhodes, C.P.A., 1600 Willow St., San Jose, Ca. 95125 - **Name of Treasurer:** Robert J. Rhodes - **Permanent Address of Treasurer:** 1600 Willow Street No. 200, San Jose, California 95125 **III. CANDIDATE/OFFICEHOLDER ONLY: IF YOU HAVE KNOWLEDGE OF ANY OTHER COMMITTEES NOT INCLUDED IN THIS CONSOLIDATED STATEMENT WHICH HAVE RECEIVED CONTRIBUTIONS OR MADE EXPENDITURES ON BEHALF OF YOUR CANDIDACY, IDENTIFY THEM IN THIS SECTION.** No additional committees are listed in this section. **VERIFICATION:** I declare under penalty of perjury that to the best of my knowledge this statement and its schedules are true, correct and complete and that I have used all reasonable diligence in their preparation.
